mcq.q: What is the place value of the 7 in 374,562?
mcq.explain: The 7 is in the ten thousands place — 70,000.
419,002 has 419 thousands — more than the others.

mcq.q: Five million, two hundred thousand in digits is…
mcq.explain: 5 millions + 200 thousands = 5,200,000.

mcq.q: What does the expanded form 600,000 + 40,000 + 800 + 3 equal?

mcq.explain: Adding 1 to 199,999 gives 200,000.
The hundreds digit is 7, so round up to 47,000.

mcq.q: In 4,872,963 the digit 8 is in the…
mcq.explain: Counting from the right: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands, ten-thousands, hundred-thousands. 8 is in hundred-thousands.

mcq.q: Write 'two million, four hundred fifty thousand' in numerals.

mcq.explain: 456,789 has the smallest hundred-thousands digit.

mcq.q: Round 27,361 to the nearest ten thousand.
mcq.explain: 7 in the thousands place rounds the ten-thousands up to 30,000.
One less than a million is 999,999.
8 ten-thousands + 5 hundreds + 3 ones.
After matching the first three digits, 745,632 has the largest hundreds digit.
Even numbers end in an even digit. 123,456 ends in 6.
5 is in the ten-thousands place — it stands for 50,000.

mcq.q: Round 248,950 to the nearest hundred thousand.
mcq.explain: 4 in the ten-thousands place rounds DOWN to 200,000.

mcq.q: Which set is in order from least to greatest?
mcq.explain: Comparing tens then ones gives the right order.

Showing each digit's place value, e.g., 3,000 + 400 + 50 + 2

choices: A group of objects that share something
mcq.explain: A set is a well-defined collection of objects that share a property.

mcq.q: Which of these items belongs to the set of Jamaican fruits?
mcq.explain: Ackee is Jamaica's national fruit.

mcq.q: How do we show a set in writing?
mcq.explain: We use curly braces, e.g., {1, 2, 3}.
An empty set (or null set) has zero elements — written { } or Ø.

mcq.q: Set A = {2, 4, 6}. Set B = {4, 6, 8}. What is A ∩ B (what's in BOTH)?
mcq.explain: Intersection: elements that appear in both sets — 4 and 6.
Classifying is sorting items into groups based on shared properties.

mcq.q: The empty set has how many members?
mcq.explain: The empty set has no members at all.

mcq.q: A ∪ B (union) means elements that are in…
mcq.explain: Union puts together everything from both sets.

mcq.q: A ∩ B (intersection) means elements that are…
mcq.explain: Intersection is what the two sets share.
Union combines without repeats.
Intersection contains only what's in both: 3 and 4.
A finite set has a countable number of members.

mcq.q: Which is an example of an empty set?
mcq.explain: No month has 32 days — the set is empty.
All elements of {1,2} are also in {1,2,3}.
Curly braces enclose the members of a set.

mcq.q: Which is the cardinal number (size) of {a, b, c, d}?
mcq.explain: Cardinality counts the elements: 4.

choices: The same number of members only
mcq.explain: Two sets are equal when they have exactly the same elements.

mcq.q: Which is a Venn diagram used for?
mcq.explain: Venn diagrams show overlaps between sets.
The universal set holds everything in the discussion.

mcq.q: How many subsets does {a, b} have?
mcq.explain: { }, {a}, {b}, {a,b} — 4 subsets in total.

flashcards.back: A group of objects sharing a property
A set with no members { } or Ø
All elements from both sets combined
Only elements that are in both sets
A set whose members are all inside another set
Overlapping circles that show how sets relate
The set of all possible elements being considered

mcq.q: A factor of a number is…
choices: A number it divides into evenly
mcq.explain: A factor divides a number with no remainder. E.g., 3 is a factor of 12.
18 = 6 × 3, so 18 is a multiple of 6.
A prime has exactly 2 factors: 1 and itself. E.g., 7 = 1 × 7.

mcq.q: Which of these is a prime number?
mcq.explain: 17 only has factors 1 and 17.

mcq.q: What is the LCM (Lowest Common Multiple) of 4 and 6?
mcq.explain: Multiples of 4: 4, 8, 12, 16... Multiples of 6: 6, 12, 18... The lowest in common is 12.
Numbers that divide 18 evenly: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18.

mcq.q: Highest Common Factor of 12 and 18 is…
mcq.explain: 6 is the largest factor common to both.

mcq.q: Lowest Common Multiple of 3 and 5 is…
mcq.explain: 3 × 5 = 15 — the smallest number both divide into.
21 = 3 × 7, so it has factors other than 1 and itself.
29 only has factors 1 and 29.
15 = 3 × 5, so it has more than two factors — composite.
1 has only one factor — itself.
Add 4 each time starting from 12.

mcq.q: Lowest Common Multiple of 6 and 8 is…
mcq.explain: 24 is the smallest number divisible by both 6 and 8.

mcq.q: Highest Common Factor of 24 and 36 is…
mcq.explain: 12 divides both 24 and 36 evenly.
By definition, 1 is neither prime nor composite.
Both share 1, 2 and 4 as factors.
Numbers ending in 0 or 5 are divisible by 5.

flashcards.back: A number that divides another evenly
The result of multiplying a number by any whole number
A number with exactly 2 factors: 1 and itself
A number with more than 2 factors
Highest Common Factor — biggest factor two numbers share
Lowest Common Multiple — smallest multiple two numbers share
Writing a number as a product of primes
Whether one number divides another with no remainder

mcq.q: In the fraction 3/5, which number is the denominator?
mcq.explain: The bottom number (5) is the denominator — the total number of equal parts.
3/6 simplifies to 1/2.
Same denominator — add the numerators: 1 + 2 = 3, giving 3/4.

mcq.q: If Kemar ate 3/8 of a bulla cake and Keisha ate 2/8, how much was eaten?
mcq.explain: 3/8 + 2/8 = 5/8 of the bulla cake.
A mixed number has a whole part and a fraction part, e.g., 3 1/2.
Same denominators, add the numerators: 1/3 + 1/3 = 2/3.
7 ÷ 4 = 1 remainder 3, so 7/4 = 1 3/4.
Divide top and bottom by 2: 6/8 = 3/4.
Common denominator 30: 25/30 vs 24/30, so 5/6 is larger.
Same numerator and denominator means a whole.

flashcards.back: Different fractions that equal the same value
Divide top and bottom by the same number until smallest form
The same bottom number — needed to add/subtract fractions

mcq.q: The decimal 0.5 is the same as…
mcq.explain: 0.5 means 5 tenths, which equals 1/2.
0.77 is larger than 0.707, 0.7, and 0.07.
The 6 is two places after the decimal, so it's 6 hundredths.
Two tenths plus three tenths = five tenths = 0.5.
0.50 after the dollar = 50 cents.
Hundredths digit (7) rounds the tenths up, giving 3.7.
Three tenths is 0.3.

mcq.q: Round 4.27 to the nearest whole number.
mcq.explain: 0.27 < 0.5, so round down to 4.

mcq.q: In 23.456 the digit 5 is in the…
mcq.explain: After the decimal: tenths, hundredths, thousandths.
0.06 has the lowest tenths digit.
Line up decimals: 4.50 + 2.25 = 6.75.

mcq.q: What is 0.9 written as a fraction?
mcq.explain: 0.9 means nine tenths = 9/10.
Line up the decimals: 7.62 + 3.40 = 11.02.
0.85 ≥ 0.5, so round up to 10.

flashcards.back: Separates whole numbers from parts smaller than 1
Look at next digit — 5 or more rounds up

mcq.explain: Ones digit is 8 (≥5), so round up to 60.
Tens digit is 4 (<5), so round down to 300.
Hundreds digit is 5, so round up to 5,000.

mcq.q: Round 12.34 to the nearest whole number.
mcq.explain: Tenths digit is 3 (<5), so round down to 12.
Rounding helps us estimate quickly when exact numbers aren't needed.

mcq.q: Which is the rule for rounding?
choices: 5 or more → round up; 4 or less → round down
mcq.explain: 5-or-more rule: look at the digit to the right; if ≥5 round up, if <5 round down.
3 < 5 → round down to 70.
5 rounds up — 685 → 690.
3 in the tens place < 5 → round down.
6 in the hundreds ≥ 5, so round up to 3,000.

mcq.q: Round 12.49 to the nearest whole number.
mcq.explain: 0.49 < 0.5 → round down to 12.

mcq.q: Round 4.5 to the nearest whole number.
mcq.explain: At exactly .5 we round up: 4.5 → 5.

mcq.q: Estimate 78 + 41 by rounding to the nearest ten.

mcq.explain: 4 < 5 → round down.

choices: To check if an answer is reasonable
mcq.explain: Rounding gives a quick estimate to check work.

mcq.q: Round J$1,247 to the nearest J$100.
mcq.explain: 4 in the tens place < 5 → round down.
7 in the hundredths ≥ 5 → round up.
9 in the tens rounds up — 999 → 1,000.

The divisor is the number you divide by — 6.

mcq.explain: 30 ÷ 5 = 6 children per team.
6 × 7 = 42, leaving 3.
100 ÷ 4 = 25 with no remainder.

mcq.explain: Dividing by 10 moves the decimal point one place left.
Dividing 0 by any non-zero number gives 0.

mcq.q: How many minutes in an hour?
mcq.explain: 60 minutes make 1 hour.
Add 12 to any pm time: 2 + 12 = 14, so 14:30.
9:15 → 11:15 is 2 hours; plus 30 minutes = 2 hours 30 minutes.
5 × 60 = 300 seconds.

mcq.q: School starts at 8:00 am and ends at 2:30 pm. How long is the school day?
mcq.explain: 8:00 to 2:30 = 6 hours 30 minutes.
Quarter to 4 = 15 minutes before 4 = 3:45.
60 × 3 = 180 minutes.
Add 12 to pm hours: 5 + 12 = 17.
1 minute = 60 seconds.
40 minutes to 8:00, then 5 more = 45 minutes.

mcq.q: How many days in a leap year?
mcq.explain: A leap year has an extra day: 366.

mcq.q: Which year is usually a leap year?
mcq.explain: Leap years happen every 4 years (with exceptions).

mcq.q: How many weeks are in a year?
mcq.explain: There are about 52 weeks in a year.
Jan, Mar, May, Jul, Aug, Oct, Dec — 7 months.
A quarter is 15 minutes; quarter past = 15 minutes after.
Half past 6 = 06:30 in the morning, or 18:30 if pm.

mcq.explain: Midnight = 12:00 am, plus 3 hours = 3:00 am.

mcq.q: How many minutes in 1/4 of an hour?
mcq.explain: 60 ÷ 4 = 15 minutes.

mcq.q: How many grams in a kilogram?

mcq.explain: Centimetres (or metres) are best for height.

mcq.q: A bag of rice weighs 5 kg. In grams that is…

mcq.explain: Most books weigh half a kilogram or so.

mcq.explain: Pools hold thousands of litres of water.

mcq.q: A child's height is best measured in…

1 kg = 1,000 g — more than 999 g.

mcq.q: A small spoon usually holds about…
mcq.explain: A teaspoon holds roughly 5 mL.
3 kg = 3,000 g, the largest.
1 cm = 10 mm, so 5 cm = 50 mm.

mcq.q: Perimeter of a rectangle 5 cm by 3 cm is…
mcq.explain: Perimeter = 2(5 + 3) = 16 cm.

mcq.q: Area of the same rectangle (5 cm × 3 cm) is…
mcq.explain: Area = length × width = 5 × 3 = 15 square cm.

mcq.q: A square has sides of 7 cm. What is its perimeter?
A square has sides of 7 cm. What is its area?
mcq.explain: Side × side = 7 × 7 = 49 cm².

mcq.q: What is the unit for area?
mcq.explain: Area is always in square units (cm², m², etc.).

Perimeter of a square with side 8 cm is…
mcq.explain: Perimeter = 4 × side = 4 × 8 = 32 cm.

mcq.q: Area of a square with side 8 cm is…
mcq.explain: Area = side × side = 8 × 8 = 64 cm².

mcq.q: Perimeter of a rectangle 9 m × 4 m is…
Area of a rectangle 9 m × 4 m is…
What unit is used for area?
mcq.explain: Area is measured in square units (cm², m², etc.).

mcq.q: What unit is used for perimeter?
mcq.explain: Perimeter is the distance around a shape, in linear units.

mcq.q: A triangle has sides 6, 8 and 10 cm. Perimeter?
A field is 50 m long and 30 m wide. Perimeter?

mcq.q: A triangle has how many sides?
mcq.explain: Tri- means three. A triangle has 3 sides and 3 angles.

mcq.q: A shape with 8 sides is a…
mcq.explain: Octo- means 8. Octagons have 8 sides — like a stop sign.

mcq.q: An angle that is exactly 90° is called…
mcq.explain: A right angle is 90° — like the corner of a book.
Acute angles are small and sharp — less than 90°.

mcq.q: A 3D shape with 6 square faces is a…
mcq.explain: A cube has 6 equal square faces — like a die.

mcq.q: How many edges does a rectangular prism have?
mcq.explain: A rectangular prism (box) has 12 edges — 4 on top, 4 on bottom, 4 vertical.

mcq.q: A 3D shape with 5 faces (1 square, 4 triangles) is a…
mcq.explain: A square pyramid has a square base and 4 triangular sides.

mcq.q: How many vertices does a cuboid have?
mcq.explain: A cuboid has 8 corners.

mcq.q: How many edges does a cube have?
mcq.explain: A cube has 12 edges.

mcq.q: A polygon with 7 sides is a…
mcq.explain: Hepta = 7, so a heptagon has 7 sides.

mcq.q: A nonagon has how many sides?
mcq.explain: Nona = 9.

mcq.q: An angle of 180° is called a…
mcq.explain: A straight angle equals 180°.

mcq.q: An angle greater than 180° but less than 360° is…
mcq.explain: Reflex angles are greater than 180°.

mcq.q: All angles in a triangle add up to…
mcq.explain: Triangle angles always sum to 180°.

mcq.q: All angles in a rectangle add up to…
mcq.explain: Quadrilateral angles total 360°.

mcq.q: A sphere has how many flat faces?
mcq.explain: A sphere is curved all over — no flat faces.

mcq.q: A cylinder has how many circular faces?
mcq.explain: A cylinder has 2 circular ends.

mcq.q: Lines that meet at a right angle are…
mcq.explain: Perpendicular lines meet at 90°.

mcq.q: A triangle with all sides equal is…
mcq.explain: Equilateral triangles have all sides and angles equal.

mcq.q: A triangle with two equal sides is…
mcq.explain: Isosceles triangles have exactly two equal sides.

flashcards.back: A closed shape with straight sides

mcq.q: A chart that uses bars to show data is called a…
mcq.explain: Bar graphs use rectangular bars of different lengths.
Pictographs use pictures/icons to represent data amounts.

mcq.q: The most common value in a data set is the…
mcq.explain: Mode = the value that appears most often.

mcq.q: The difference between the highest and lowest values is the…
mcq.explain: Range = highest − lowest.

mcq.q: Probability of rolling a 6 on a regular die is…
mcq.explain: One favourable outcome (6) out of 6 possible outcomes = 1/6.
5 appears most often.
The middle value when sorted is 6.
With 4 numbers, the median is (4+6)/2 = 5.
Highest − lowest = 30 − 12 = 18.

mcq.q: Probability of an impossible event is…
mcq.explain: Impossible means it cannot happen — probability 0.

mcq.q: Rolling a die, the chance of an odd number is…
mcq.explain: 3 of 6 sides are odd: 3/6 = 1/2.

mcq.q: In a bag of 6 reds and 4 blues, picking red has probability…
mcq.explain: 6 reds out of 10 total = 6/10 = 3/5.

mcq.q: On a fair coin, chance of tails is…
mcq.explain: Two equally likely outcomes give 1/2.

mcq.q: A line graph is best for showing…
mcq.explain: Line graphs show how values change over time.

mcq.q: A pie chart is best for…
mcq.explain: Pie charts split a circle by category share.

mcq.q: In a bar chart, the height of bars shows…
mcq.explain: Tall bars represent larger quantities.
6 appears three times — the most.

Chart using pictures to show data
Chart using a line to show change over time

mcq.q: Which food group gives us energy quickly?
mcq.explain: Carbohydrates (rice, bread, yam) provide quick energy.

mcq.q: Which is a good Jamaican source of protein?
mcq.explain: Ackee and saltfish contain protein — saltfish is especially rich.
Fruits and vegetables like callaloo and mango are rich in vitamins.

mcq.q: Which nutrient helps build strong bones and teeth?
mcq.explain: Calcium, found in milk and cheese, builds strong bones and teeth.

mcq.q: Too much of which can cause tooth decay?
mcq.explain: Sugar feeds bacteria in the mouth, which damages teeth.

mcq.q: How many glasses of water should a child drink daily?
mcq.explain: Around 6–8 glasses keeps the body hydrated.

mcq.q: Carbohydrates are mainly used by the body for…
mcq.explain: Carbs are the body's main fuel for energy.
Dairy foods like cheese, milk and yoghurt are rich in calcium.

mcq.q: Vitamin C helps the body to…
mcq.explain: Vitamin C supports the immune system and wound healing.

mcq.q: Which Jamaican fruit is a great source of vitamin C?
mcq.explain: Guava has very high amounts of vitamin C.

mcq.q: Iron in food helps to make…
mcq.explain: Iron helps blood carry oxygen by forming red blood cells.
Fibre keeps the digestive system moving smoothly.

mcq.q: Eating too much fatty food can lead to…
mcq.explain: Diets high in unhealthy fats can damage the heart over time.
Balanced meals contain carbs, protein, fruits and vegetables.

mcq.q: Which drink is best for daily hydration?
mcq.explain: Water is the best, healthiest fluid for hydration.
Hygiene habits keep germs from spreading and you healthy.
Cycling raises your heart rate — it's good aerobic exercise.

mcq.q: Lack of vitamin D often leads to weak…
mcq.explain: Vitamin D helps absorb calcium for strong bones.
Saltfish is a strong source of protein.

mcq.q: Which cooking method keeps more nutrients?
choices: Boiling for a very long time
mcq.explain: Steaming uses gentle heat and saves more vitamins.

flashcards.back: Stores energy (oil, butter) — needed in small amounts
Keeps body systems working (from fruits & veg)
Eating the right amounts from all food groups

mcq.q: What is at the start of every food chain?
mcq.explain: The sun provides energy that plants use to make food. Energy flow begins there.

mcq.q: An animal that eats only plants is a…
mcq.explain: Herbivores eat only plants — like cows and goats.

mcq.q: In a food web, arrows point from…
mcq.explain: Arrows show energy flow — from prey to predator.
Fungi and bacteria break down dead matter.

mcq.q: Grass → Goat → Mongoose. The mongoose is a…
mcq.explain: Goat eats grass (primary); mongoose eats goat (secondary consumer).

mcq.q: If all plants were removed, what would happen?
mcq.explain: Plants are the base of the food chain — without them, everything collapses.

mcq.q: Producers in a food chain are…
mcq.explain: Producers (plants) make their own food using sunlight.

choices: Animals that eat other living things
mcq.explain: Consumers cannot make food, so they eat other organisms.

mcq.q: A snake eating a frog that ate an insect is a…
mcq.explain: A consumer at the third level (third in line) is a tertiary consumer.
Decomposers like fungi recycle nutrients from dead organisms.

mcq.q: In a food web, arrows show…
choices: Energy moving from prey to predator
mcq.explain: Arrows go from what is eaten to who eats it (energy flow).

choices: Fish, corals, algae and many others
mcq.explain: Reefs are ecosystems with many living things sharing the area.

mcq.q: A sea-grass eaten by a turtle eaten by a shark — the shark is a…
mcq.explain: The shark is the top predator — no one eats it in this chain.

mcq.q: Energy at each step of a food chain mostly…
mcq.explain: Most energy is lost as heat — only a small amount passes up.

mcq.q: What happens if mongooses eat too many lizards?
mcq.explain: Heavy predation reduces prey numbers.

mcq.q: A 'food web' differs from a food chain because it shows…
mcq.explain: A web shows how many chains overlap in nature.

mcq.q: Which organism turns sunlight into food?
mcq.explain: Algae are plant-like producers that photosynthesise.
Manatees graze on sea grass — pure herbivores.

mcq.q: If a key producer dies out, the ecosystem will likely…
mcq.explain: Losing producers reduces the food and energy available to all.

mcq.q: Bacteria and fungi are important because they…
mcq.explain: They break down dead material so nutrients return to the soil.

mcq.q: Which is a major staple food grown in Jamaica?
mcq.explain: Yam is a major staple grown across Jamaica — Trelawny is famous for it.

mcq.q: Farmers need what to grow good crops?
mcq.explain: Good soil, water, and sunlight are basic needs for crops.

mcq.q: Jamaica's Blue Mountain region is famous for growing…
mcq.explain: Blue Mountain Coffee is world-famous, grown at high altitude.
Rotating crops replenishes different nutrients in the soil.

mcq.q: A disease that affected Jamaican banana crops is…
mcq.explain: Black sigatoka is a fungal disease that damages banana leaves.
Organic farming avoids synthetic pesticides and fertilisers.

mcq.q: Yam, cassava and dasheen are grown for their…
mcq.explain: These crops are root crops, eaten for their underground parts.

mcq.q: Coffee in Jamaica is most famous from the…
mcq.explain: Blue Mountain coffee grows on the high mountains.

choices: Planting the same crop every year
mcq.explain: Rotation keeps soil rich and breaks pest cycles.

mcq.q: Compost is good for soil because it adds…
mcq.explain: Compost returns nutrients from old plants to feed new ones.

mcq.q: Which is a pest of crops?
mcq.explain: Locusts can swarm and destroy crops.
Irrigation supplies water to fields when rain isn't enough.

choices: A small home garden of food
mcq.explain: Kitchen gardens provide vegetables and herbs for the family.

mcq.q: Which crop is a legume that adds nitrogen to soil?
mcq.explain: Beans (legumes) host bacteria that fix nitrogen in the soil.

mcq.q: Which Jamaican crop is grown for its juice and sugar?
mcq.explain: Sugar cane is pressed for its sweet juice that becomes sugar.

choices: Covering soil with straw or leaves to keep moisture
mcq.explain: Mulch keeps soil moist and reduces weeds.
Monoculture is the practice of growing one crop year after year.
4-H clubs teach farming, leadership and other practical skills.

choices: Controlling temperature and protecting from pests
mcq.explain: Greenhouses provide warm, protected conditions all year.

mcq.q: Why do farmers test their soil?
choices: To know which nutrients are missing
mcq.explain: Soil tests help farmers add the right fertiliser.

mcq.explain: A wind vane points into the wind to show direction.
